assertion.text: Erdős Problem #208: declared status 'open'. Formalized: yes. Let $s_1<s_2<\cdots$ be the sequence of squarefree numbers. Is it true that, for any $\epsilon>0$ and large $n$,\[s_{n+1}-s_n \ll_\epsilon s_n^{\epsilon}?\]Is it true that\[s_{n+1}-s_n \leq (1+o(1))\frac{\pi^2}{6}\frac{\log s_n}{\log\log s_n}?\] Current best: Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er51} showed that there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[s_{n+1}-s_n > (1+o(1))\frac{\pi^2}{6}\frac{\log s_n}{\log\log s_n},\]so this bound would be the best possible. Filaseta and Trifonov \cite{FiTr92} proved an upper bound of $s_n^{1/5+o(1)}$. Prize: no. OEIS: A005117, A076259. Tags: number theory.
